macro_context.market_sentiment: Overall crypto market sentiment is cautious-to-fearful. Bitcoin tested $75K support levels over the weekend, pulling back from a Friday high near $78K. DeFi suffered a $14B exodus following the KelpDAO hack, adding risk-off pressure. Despite geopolitical headwinds (US-Iran tensions, Strait of Hormuz), BTC has largely held the $74K–$76K range, suggesting underlying bid from institutional flows.
macro_context.btc_dominance: BTC dominance is approximately 57–58.5% (sources vary: 57.0% per TradingView Hub, 58.5% per CoinMarketCap as of Apr 20). Trend is modestly down from a June 2025 peak of ~65%, but firmly in 'Bitcoin Season' territory. CMC Altcoin Season Index sits at 34/100, confirming BTC dominance. Altcoin rotation has not begun in earnest.
macro_context.total_market_cap: Total crypto market cap is approximately $2.50–2.62T as of April 20–21, 2026. The range reflects intraday volatility driven by Iran-related risk-off and partial recovery. Market cap is down from highs but holding above the $2.5T psychological floor.
macro_context.fear_greed_index: Fear & Greed Index: 29 (Fear) as of April 19–20, 2026 (sources: feargreedmeter.com, milkroad.com, bitdegree.org). Up 2 points from the prior day. The index briefly hit 11 (Extreme Fear) in early April per HedgeCo Insights. Historically, sub-30 readings have marked medium-term accumulation windows.
macro_context.fed_outlook: No major Federal Reserve policy shift announced in April 2026. Macro backdrop remains uncertain with US-Iran geopolitical tensions driving some risk-off flows. Crypto's correlation to equities remains elevated; any Fed pivot toward rate cuts would be a positive catalyst for BTC. No immediate Fed meeting catalyst this week.
macro_context.institutional_flows: Strongly positive. US spot BTC ETFs recorded $996M in net weekly inflows (week ending Apr 17), the best week since Jan 16 and the third consecutive positive week. Total cumulative ETF inflows exceeded $57.74B with AUM at $101.45B. BlackRock IBIT attracted $906M that week alone; Morgan Stanley's newly launched MSBT added $71M in its first full trading week (launched Apr 8). Single-day peak: $663M on Apr 18, largest since Jan 15. MicroStrategy (Strategy) surpassed BlackRock as the largest institutional BTC holder, holding 300,000+ BTC. Fidelity made a $25M direct corporate treasury BTC purchase in April.
macro_context.regulatory: SEC and CFTC issued a joint comprehensive interpretation (Mar 17) clarifying federal securities law application to crypto assets, following a formal MOU (Mar 11). SEC Chair Paul Atkins submitted 'Regulation Crypto' proposal to the White House OIRA in early April — focused on Securities Act fundraising exemptions, aimed at reducing ambiguity for token issuers. SEC also issued a statement (Apr 13) not objecting to certain decentralized trading UI providers operating without broker registration. Senate Banking Committee advancing the CLARITY Act in late April, which would permanently codify XRP's commodity status. Overall regulatory trajectory is constructive and moving toward clarity rather than restriction.
key_events: Bitcoin Conference 2026 approaching — historical data (2019–2025) shows BTC tends to underperform in the week and month following the conference, presenting potential sell-the-news risk
US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve architecture due from Trump administration before July 22, 2026 — pending Congressional legislation via likely NDAA pathway
Senate Banking Committee markup of CLARITY Act expected late April 2026 — could permanently codify commodity status for multiple crypto assets
KelpDAO DeFi hack triggered $14B TVL exodus from DeFi protocols — may drive rotation toward BTC as perceived safe haven within crypto
US-Iran tensions with Strait of Hormuz closure risk — macro geopolitical wildcard affecting all risk assets
Morgan Stanley MSBT ETF launched April 8 — adds new institutional distribution channel for BTC exposure

ticker_news.headline: Bitcoin bounces above $76,000 as DeFi suffers $14B exodus after KelpDAO hack
Bitcoin ETFs attract $996M weekly inflow streak — three consecutive weeks positive, IBIT leads with $906M
Bitcoin funding rates hit most negative since 2023 — history suggests market bottom may be in
Bitcoin quantum debate splits community — Adam Back pushes optional quantum-resistant upgrades vs. BIP-361 forced migration proposal
Satoshi-era whale moves 1,000 BTC ($74M) to exchanges; MicroStrategy absorbs 17x that volume in 48 hours, stabilizing BTC at $74,100

on_chain_signals.whale_activity: Mixed signals. Bullish: whale addresses holding 1,000+ BTC grew from 2,082 (Dec 2025) to 2,140, net-buying ~270,000 BTC over 30 days — the largest accumulation since 2013 per CryptoQuant. Exchange reserves hit a 7-year low at 2.21M BTC (5.88% of supply). Bearish: large holders (1,000–10,000 BTC wallets) have flipped to aggressive distribution, removing ~188,000 BTC in what analysts call one of the most aggressive distribution cycles on record. Hourly exchange inflows spiked to 11,000 BTC on Apr 15 (highest since late Dec 2025), driven by large Binance deposits exceeding 1,000 BTC. A Satoshi-era wallet moved 1,000 BTC ($74M) to exchanges. Net read: accumulation trend intact at macro level, but short-term distribution pressure near $76K resistance is real.
on_chain_signals.funding_rates: Perpetual futures funding rates are deeply negative, around -0.005% on a 7-day moving average as of mid-April — the most negative since 2023 (CoinDesk, Apr 16). This indicates heavy short positioning in perps markets even as spot price has risen from ~$65K to ~$75K. Deeply negative funding historically coincides with local bottoms (March 2020, mid-2021, FTX collapse Nov 2022) and sets up conditions for a short squeeze. Bullish contrarian signal: crowded shorts are potential fuel for an upside move.
on_chain_signals.exchange_flows: Net exchange flows are broadly negative (coins leaving exchanges = bullish accumulation signal). Monthly average since March 1: -1,640 BTC/day, indicating sustained withdrawal trend. Bitcoin 'Free Float' on centralized exchanges has hit a 10-year low by mid-April. However, spike in inflows around the $76K resistance level (Apr 15: 11,000 BTC/hour) suggests large holders used the rally to distribute. ETF inflows ($996M weekly) are absorbing this sell pressure so far. Net read: structural accumulation continues, but watch for additional exchange inflow spikes near resistance as a warning sign.

development_activity: Bitcoin Core repository (github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin) remains actively developed as of April 2026: 38,907 total commits, last updated April 16, 2026. Currently 345 open pull requests and 16 issues marked 'help wanted'. Active work includes GUI improvements, reindexing functionality, validation improvements, and P2P features. Bitcoin Core v31.0 released in April 2026, a major node software update enhancing transaction privacy, fee efficiency, and network performance. The quantum-resistance debate is active at the protocol design level (Adam Back vs. BIP-361 proposal), indicating healthy long-term security planning. Development momentum is steady and mature — Bitcoin's low commit velocity relative to altcoins reflects deliberate, conservative protocol design philosophy rather than stagnation.
upcoming_catalysts: 1) Bitcoin Conference 2026 (imminent) — historical pattern: BTC often rallies into conference then underperforms in following weeks; sell-the-news risk. 2) US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve architecture due before July 22, 2026 — Trump administration committed but facing legislative hurdles; positive if Congressional action accelerates. 3) CLARITY Act Senate Banking Committee markup (late April 2026) — broader crypto commodity classification could boost market confidence. 4) Quantum-resistance BIP-361 debate resolution — no near-term impact but long-term significance for network security. 5) Short squeeze potential — deeply negative funding rates (-0.005% 7DMA) with rising spot price creates conditions for a rapid upside move if bearish perp positions unwind. 6) BTC Core v31.0 post-release network adoption — miners and node operators upgrading over coming weeks. 7) Geopolitical wildcard: US-Iran Strait of Hormuz situation — de-escalation could be risk-on catalyst for BTC.
